sections.paragraph: (i.q. لَكِسُ “obstinate,” i.e. hard to be captured), [Lachish], pr.n. of a fortified town ( Isa 36:2 2Ch 11:9) in the plain country, of the tribe of Judah, which had formerly been a royal city of the Canaanites, Jos 10:3 12:11 15:39 Neh 11:30 Jer 34:7 Mic 1:13. [Derived in Thes. from the unused root. לָכַשׁ; and the meaning suggested, “smitten,” “taken,” “captured,” or else the one just mentioned.]
